public_functions.behavior_description: Initialize the eGTM for 3-phase complementary PWM and a trigger output, configure TMADC trigger routing, set GPIOs, and prepare watchdog. Algorithm: 1) Enable the eGTM clocks required by the ATOM and DTM blocks. 2) Initialize a unified PWM configuration structure with defaults and set the operating frequency to the required PWM frequency. 3) Configure a first PWM group for three synchronous channels (base + two sync) using the same ATOM submodule channels in consecutive order. Set the alignment to center-aligned for this group, enable complementary outputs through the DTM, and set the deadtime to the requested value by converting the deadtime duration into DTM clock ticks (using the eGTM/DTM clock) and applying those values to both rising and falling delays. Configure the high-side output polarity as active-high and the complementary (low-side) output polarity as active-low. 4) Assign output pins for all configured PWM outputs (primary and complementary) using the generic eGTM pin-map API, and apply appropriate output pad modes/drive settings. 5) Configure a second PWM context or group for a single channel to act as a 50% duty, edge-aligned trigger at the same base frequency; assign its output pin and set its alignment to edge-aligned. 6) Route the trigger channel to the ADC trigger bus using the eGTM trigger-to-ADC API so that the ADC sampling is synchronized to the PWM timebase; the route selection chooses the appropriate internal trigger line to the ADC trigger input as per device routing. 7) Initialize the TMADC module with default configuration and start the module; channel-specific mapping remains to be added later when available. 8) Configure the heartbeat GPIO pin as a push-pull output (initial level arbitrary) using the port driver. 9) Initialize/configure the watchdog using the WTU driver as required by the system policy. 10) Start the PWM outputs in a synchronized manner so that the three-phase group and the trigger channel start coherently. Store any driver handles and local state (such as current duty array) needed for runtime updates.

public_functions.signature: void updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty(float32 *requestDuty /* length=3, phase order: U, V, W */)
public_functions.behavior_description: Update the three phase duties immediately and toggle the heartbeat. Algorithm: 1) Read three requested duty values from the provided array (phase order consistent with initialization). 2) Optionally clamp each duty to the valid range [0.0, 1.0] and store them into an internal duty buffer. 3) Apply the new duty values to all configured PWM channels in the 3‑phase group using an immediate multi-channel duty update so the change takes effect without waiting for a global sync. 4) Toggle the heartbeat GPIO pin to indicate the periodic service of this update routine. The function does not change frequency, alignment, phase, or deadtime. Note: The heartbeat toggle period is determined by how often this function is called; to achieve a fixed 500 ms period, ensure the call rate matches or implement an internal timer/counter to enforce a 500 ms toggle interval regardless of call rate.
